title: Warm Wonton-Style Ukrainian Egg Tagine
description: A sweet and savory Ukrainian dish featuring spherified eggs, mustard flavors, and the comfort of star anise, perfect for a cozy brunch.
instructions: In a small saucepan, whisk together heavy cream, Dijon mustard, honey, cinnamon, star anise, and salt over medium heat.
Bring the mixture to a simmer and cook, stirring occasionally, until thickened to a syrupy consistency, about 10 minutes.
Remove the saucepan from the heat and stir in the xanthan gum until dissolved.
Strain the mixture into a clean bowl and allow to cool to room temperature.
Using a spherification kit, drop small balls of the mixture into a water bath and spherify the eggs.
Remove the spherified eggs from the water bath and place on a wire rack to drain excess water.
In a large ceramic tagine, melt the butter over medium heat.
Add the spherified eggs to the tagine and cook, stirring occasionally, until they start to brown and caramelize, about 5 minutes.
Stir in the sugar glass syrup and cook for an additional 2 minutes.
Season with chopped fresh dill and serve warm.
Use a spoon to break up any spherified eggs that have burst or deflated during cooking.
Serve immediately, garnished with additional fresh dill if desired.
